topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias stared at the printed page, the ink seeming to mock him. Every carefully chosen adjective, every significant turning point in his life, was a calculated stroke of a pen he had never seen. His hands, usually steady when signing contracts, were trembling slightly as he traced the fabricated narrative. "This is absurd," he muttered, the sound thin and brittle in the quiet study. He slammed the document onto the mahogany desk, the sharp crack echoing in the room. He rose abruptly, pacing the length of the Persian rug, his jaw tight enough to crack enamel. He needed answers, and he needed them now, because the very foundation of who he believed himself to be had been systematically dismantled.

[story 2]

The email landed in Clara’s inbox, stark white against the dark background of her screen. It was a professionally formatted document detailing her entire life—her triumphs, her failures, her supposed motivations—written in a voice that was chillingly familiar yet utterly alien. Clara reread the final paragraph three times, her breath catching in her throat. A flush crept up her neck, hot and undeniable. She gripped the mouse so tightly her knuckles went white. "Who authorized this?" she demanded into the empty room, her voice tight with controlled fury. She stood up, walking to the window, staring out at the indifferent city lights, feeling a profound sense of being utterly unseen, cataloged, and misrepresented.

[story 3]

When Marcus finally saw the biography, the color drained from his face. He didn't move for a long moment, simply standing frozen in the doorway of the library. His eyes darted from the glossy paper to the empty space where he expected his own story to reside. A low growl escaped his chest, a sound more animal than human. He snatched the document, his fingers crumpling the edges before he could stop himself. He began tearing at the pages, the sound of ripping paper loud and violent. "This isn't mine," he hissed, his voice shaking with suppressed rage. He threw the shreds onto the floor, kicking them with a frustrated stomp, his entire body vibrating with a furious need to reclaim ownership over his existence.
